identity.name: shell_cli_extension_bashly_bats
identity.title: Amend kernel with shell/CLI extension first slice: Bashly + Bats

objective: Adopt the shell/CLI extension first amendment slice for Bashly implementation projection and Bats verification projection as a kernel review packet grounded in the existing metadata metaschema lane.
constraints: The transport bundle payload must remain byte-identical during packet generation.
The canonical metadata model remains the authority baseline.
Shell assets remain projection-only and do not become semantic authority.
The first amendment slice is limited to Bashly as implementation target and Bats as verification consumer.
The scm.pattern packet lane remains a downstream operational carrier and must not define shell semantics.
Packet input and deliverable kinds must remain aligned with the approved ChatGPT packet family registry.
All packet refs must remain repo-relative.
ChatGPT may author packet artifacts only and may not realize them.
assumptions: The codex_home proposal slice is transported into kernel as a validated bundle basis.
The landed metadata metaschema lane remains the upstream semantic authority for this extension slice.
Human review remains authoritative before any kernel adoption beyond the packet lane.
acceptance_criteria: The shell/CLI extension bundle is included unchanged in the transport bundle.
The packet emits the canonical machine and human artifact family.
Packet input and deliverable roles are traceable to the approved ChatGPT packet family registry.
The packet validates against the live packet schemas and trust boundary.
The packet remains review-only until an authoritative human decision exists.
The Bashly implementation slice and Bats verification slice remain bounded as the only amendment-facing backend scope in this packet.
review_criteria: The canonical metadata model remains authoritative.
Shell assets remain derived projection artifacts.
The Bashly and Bats slice remains bounded and non-authoritative.
The bundle payload remains unchanged from the codex_home proposal basis.
